name: Cranberry Cheesecake with Walnut Crust
recipeIngredient: 3 packages (8 oz. each) cream cheese, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
2. In a small bowl combine crumbs, walnuts and melted butter.
3. Press the crumb mixture firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch spring-form pan. Bake until golden, 5 to 6 minutes; remove from oven; cool slightly.
4. Spread with cranberry sauce; set aside.
5. Reduce oven temperature to 300 degrees.
6. In a large bowl beat cream cheese, sugar and flour until smooth. Beat in eggs, sour cream and vanilla until well blended.
7. Spread evenly over the cranberry sauce.
8. Bake until a knife inserted 1-1/2-inches from the edge comes out clean, about 1 hour.
9. Turn off oven; leave cheesecake in oven with door ajar until top is firm to the touch, about 30 minutes.
10. Cool on a wire rack, about 1 hour. Cover and refrigerate until cold, about 4 hours.
11. Just before serving, remove cheesecake from pan to a serving plate. Garnish with sugared cranberries, kiwi slices and mint leaves, if desired.
NOTE: To make sugared cranberries: In a small bowl place a small amount of white corn syrup. Add fresh cranberries, a few at a time, turning to coat; then place in a bowl of granulated sugar. Using a fork, remove cranberries from sugar and place on a plate until ready to use.
